evals.prompt: Create a proctmux.yaml for an external user with a Node app that has a web dev server, an API server, and a worker. Use pnpm, add ./node_modules/.bin to PATH, autostart web and api, and make the worker manual. Use categories so they can filter frontend/backend/worker.
evals.expected_output: A valid proctmux.yaml using procs with shell commands, add_path, autostart, categories, and no ignored legacy fields.

evals.prompt: I need a proctmux config for docker compose where stop sends SIGINT, waits 10 seconds, and then runs docker compose down as cleanup. Also turn on unified mode focus hiding and keep ctrl+left/ctrl+right/ctrl+w bindings documented.
evals.expected_output: A config with procs.<name>.stop, stop_timeout_ms, on_kill argv list, layout.hide_process_list_when_unfocused, and keybinding focus options.

evals.prompt: An external user pasted a proctmux.yaml that uses stale options: enable_mouse, signal_server, general.kill_existing_session, style.color_level, and style.placeholder_terminal_bg_color. Review and fix it without telling them to inspect proctmux internals. Keep the real process definitions intact.
evals.expected_output: A corrected config or patch that removes ignored legacy fields while preserving active options and process entries.
